title: RNA Stable Isotope Probing Experimental Set Up
author: Julie Huber
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.iuzcex6

steps.instruction: To an evacuated, acid-washed and sterile, stoppered 500 mL pyrex bottle, add 8.83 mL of 12C or 13C DIC (600 mM solution, sterile, in serum vials) using a 10 mL syringe with a filter on it. Do the 12C first. Final concentration = 10mM. This concentration will depend on your experimental design.

Using a peristaltic pump, fill each bottle to 530mL (premarked) using ~60-100 mL/minute. Fill 12C first, then 13C. Change needle in between. Change tubing between isotopes. If bottles look like they are not filling it might be due to gas build up. Release pressure with a needle.
Add 1 to 1.5mL of 10% HCl to each bottle with a 1 mL syringe, checking pH repeatedly until you reach desired pH (e.g. pH < 6.5).
Add 20 mL of hydrogen gas to each bottle using small hydrogen bottle of 100% hydrogen with a 60mL syringe with a stopcock.
Incubate bottles on their sides for appropriate period of time at chosen temperatures (e.g. 30 °C for 36 hrs, 55 °C for 18-24 hrs, and 80 °C for 9-18 hrs).

To end the experiment, place a needle attached to peristaltic pump tubing and also a vent needle in the stopper, turn upside down in a ring stand and filter fluid through a 0.22 uM Sterivex at ~60 mL/min. Use 12C tubing for controls, 13C tubing for experiment; watch which isotope as well. Run pump backward.
When all fluid has been filtered, fill Sterivex with filter-sterilized RNAlater, cap with Medex caps, and store at 4ºC for 24 hours and then move to -80ºC.
To clean tubing between experiments, use peristaltic pump and a beaker to pass DI/MilliQ, 10% HCL, MilliQ, 70% EtOH, and then MilliQ through tubing, then empty and dry.
